3 cuafropolitan asacolumbia Follow “THE SPIRIT OF AFRICA,” it’s hard to describe in just words. It’s faith, it’s family——brother and sister, father and son, it’s black boy joy, it’s the progression of the diaspora and a home we all know, it’s cultural legacy expounded upon itself, it’s an immigrant’s courage, it’s a choice to embrace ancestry, it’s everything and anything that has meant our evolution as a continent, and yet, how we can come back together and celebrate one another as Africans. …

This Black History Month, we’re featuring Afro-Palestinians. Afro-Palestinian history is Palestinian history. After the 1948 Nakba, Afro-Palestinians experienced forced displacement with thousands of Afro-Palestinians forced to flee East Jerusalem. The Afro-Palestinian story is deeply intertwined and interwoven with the movement for a liberated Palestine. ❤️‍🔥 moonlightkn1ght   50w @yassir_lester ❤️ Reply monotropism0   56w @afrocol

Everyone should be CopWatch trained 🫡 (especially if you’re white) This training will be virtual, RSVP link in our bio.  CopWatching is part of a lineage of community self-defense tactics that goes back to the Black Panthers and the Young Lords. The Justice Committee describes CopWatch as “an act of self defense rooted in love and solidarity.” Its goal is to prevent, deescalate, and expose police violence/ misconduct.

Join Columbia AAPI Interboard for a screening of Mohanad Yaqubi’s film, Reel No. 21 AKA Restoring Solidarity! R21 is a reflection on a collection of twenty 16mm films, safeguarded in Tokyo by the Japanese solidarity movement with Palestine. This can create hidden weak points where stress quietly builds over time, especially under heavy loads, strong winds, or ground movement. Out of ~200 instructor jobs in a regular year, we know of only ~10 that have been appointed. International students say that Columbia Univeristy’s response to the DHS raids and visa revocations is gravely inadequate and left many feeling unsafe. Columbia’s own repressive tactics of empowering Public Safety officers to brutalize students has erased what little trust we have left in this institution. At the bargaining table, we are prepared to fight hard for our rights as international students
